title: pescatoria russeliana
intro: Pescatoria russeliana, also known as the "fish poison tree," is a plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant contains a toxic alkaloid called pescatorin, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Pescatoria russeliana are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Pescatoria russeliana can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, although its use should be approached with caution due to the plant's toxicity.
